frame_analyses.description: Preparation is underway, instruments and materials are being arranged.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Ensure all instruments are ready before starting.

frame_analyses.description: First instrument enters the field, needle is being loaded.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Ensure secure grip on needle while loading.

frame_analyses.description: Needle is being passed through the first mark on the Penrose drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Aim for minimal deviation from the center of the marks.

frame_analyses.description: Surgeon's knot (double throw) is being performed.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Ensure the first throw is secure; use a double throw for this step.

frame_analyses.description: Single throw is being performed; hand is switched.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Switch hands between throws to maintain knot security.

frame_analyses.description: Final throw is completed, hand is switched again.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Ensure the third throw secures the knot in place.

frame_analyses.description: Both suture ends are being cut.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Make sure suture ends are cut flush with the knot to avoid loose ends.

knot_assessments.notes: Third throw secures knot; hand switched again.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: No visible gaps; good slit closure.

technique_summary: Task performed efficiently with minimal deviations and secure knot tying. Proper hand switching and good closure of the Penrose drain slit were observed.
improvement_suggestions: Ensure suture is aligned for minimal deviation at the start.
Maintain consistent force to avoid inconsistencies in throw security.
strengths: Effective hand switching improving knot security.
Secure and complete knot tying with minimal suture deviation.
